headline: Morgan Stanley raises Intel target but prefers these chip stocks more
summary: Investing.com -- Morgan Stanley raised its price target on Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) to $56 from $41, citing stronger server demand and higher expected earnings, but said memory chipmakers Micron and SanDisk offer better risk-reward for investors looking to play the AI-driven surge in processor demand.

headline: Morgan Stanley sees agentic AI widening chip spending beyond graphics processors to CPUs
summary: Morgan Stanley said increasingly autonomous artificial intelligence could boost demand for central processing units (CPUs), ‌reshape data center buildout and widen investment beyond ‌the graphic chips that have dominated the AI boom so far. "As AI ​transitions from generation to autonomous action, the computing bottleneck is shifting towards CPU and memory, driving a step-change in general-purpose compute intensity," Morgan Stanley said in a note on ‌Sunday, adding that ⁠demand for graphic processing units (GPUs) remains strong. Morgan Stanley estimates agentic AI could add $32.5–60 billion ⁠to a data-centre CPU market already exceeding $100 billion by 2030.

content.title: Morgan Stanley sees agentic AI widening chip spending beyond graphics processors to CPUs
content.summary: Morgan Stanley said increasingly autonomous artificial intelligence could boost demand for central processing units (CPUs), ‌reshape data center buildout and widen investment beyond ‌the graphic chips that have dominated the AI boom so far. "As AI ​transitions from generation to autonomous action, the computing bottleneck is shifting towards CPU and memory, driving a step-change in general-purpose compute intensity," Morgan Stanley said in a note on ‌Sunday, adding that ⁠demand for graphic processing units (GPUs) remains strong. Morgan Stanley estimates agentic AI could add $32.5–60 billion ⁠to a data-centre CPU market already exceeding $100 billion by 2030.

headline: Intel Stock Is Falling Hard. What’s Pausing the Recent Rally.
summary: Shares of  Intel  fell sharply Monday, putting an end—or at least a pause—to the chip stock’s hot recent run.  Entering Monday, Intel stock had risen 11 of the past 12 sessions dating back to March 31.  Shares remain up 48% so far in April and almost 250% from a year ago, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

content.title: Intel Stock Is Falling Hard. What’s Pausing the Recent Rally.
